name: Metoclopramide
indications:
## Main indications

Antiemetic, antinauseant.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: 10 mg slow IV every 6–8 h PRN.

pediatrico.presentation: Tablets: 10 mg; Pediatric drops: 2 mg/ml (0,1 mg/drop); Adult drops: 5 mg/ml; Ampoules 2 ml: 5 mg/ml
pediatrico.dose: Children: 0,4 - 0,8 mg/kg/day every 6 h; adults: 10 - 15 mg/dose every 6 h. For chemotherapy: IV - PO: 1 - 2 mg/kg/dose, every 2 - 4 h for 2 to 5 doses, if nausea and/or vomiting continue: 0,5 mg/kg or 30 mg; every 4 - 6 h for 5 days. Postoperative: children: 0,1 - 0,2 mg/kg/dose every 6 - 8 h; > 14 years and adults: 10 mg every 6 - 8 h. Maximum dose is that preceding extrapyramidal effects.
pediatrico.compatibility: Incompatible with cephalothin and sodium bicarbonate.
pediatrico.notes: Use with caution and reduce dose in renal insufficiency, hypertension or depression. Antimuscarinics and opioid analgesics antagonize metoclopramide effect on the gastrointestinal tract; antipsychotics increase risk of extrapyramidal effects. Metoclopramide increases effects of paracetamol and aspirin.

neonatal.dose: Restricted use; 0,1 mg/kg per NICU protocol.

stability:
## General

- Use after withdrawal.

## Pediatric guide

- Discard remainder once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Extrapyramidal symptoms (more common in children and young adults, especially after high IV doses), seizures, neuroleptic malignant syndrome. Occasionally: somnolence, restlessness, depression, diarrhea.
